CustomerProfile.title: CustomerProfile
CustomerProfile.description: Stores information specific to a customer account, linked to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomerProfile entity.

firebaseAuthUid.description: Unique identifier from Firebase Authentication, linking this profile to an authenticated user. (Relationship: Firebase Auth 1:1 CustomerProfile)

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the customer's profile image.

loyaltyPoints.description: Current loyalty points accumulated by the customer for reward programs.

referralCode.description: A unique code assigned to this customer for referring new users.

referredByCustomerId.description: Reference to the CustomerProfile ID of the customer who referred this user. (Relationship: CustomerProfile 1:N CustomerProfile)

walletBalance.description: Current balance in the customer's in-app wallet, usable for purchases.

favoriteMenuItemIds.description: An array of IDs of menu items favorited by the customer. (Relationship: CustomerProfile N:N MenuItem)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the customer profile was last updated.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Represents a physical address associated with a customer, used for delivery or billing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the CustomerProfile this address belongs to. (Relationship: CustomerProfile 1:N Address)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address, if applicable.

stateProvince.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal or ZIP code of the address.

label.description: A user-defined label for the address (e.g., 'Home', 'Work').

isDefault.description: Indicates if this is the customer's default address.

latitude.description: Geographical latitude of the address for location-based services.

longitude.description: Geographical longitude of the address for location-based services.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the address was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the address was last updated.

Restaurant.title: Restaurant
Restaurant.description: Represents a restaurant managed by the DineFlow Global platform. (Minimal structure for demonstrating relationships.)

id.description: Unique identifier for the Restaurant entity.

name.description: The official name of the restaurant.

description.description: A brief description of the restaurant.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the restaurant was added to the platform.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the restaurant information was last updated.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a single item available on a restaurant's menu. (Minimal structure for demonstrating relationships.)

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the Restaurant that offers this menu item. (Relationship: Restaurant 1:N MenuItem)

name.description: The name of the menu item.

price.description: The price of the menu item.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's order from a specific restaurant, including order history. (Updated for customer linkage.)

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the Restaurant from which the order was placed. (Relationship: Restaurant 1:N Order)

customerId.description: Reference to the CustomerProfile who placed this order, for order history. (Relationship: CustomerProfile 1:N Order)

orderItemIds.description: An array of IDs of the OrderItem entities included in this order. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including taxes and fees.

currency.description: The three-letter currency code (e.g., USD, EUR) for the order total.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single item within a customer's order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem ordered. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the menu item ordered.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the menu item at the time of order.

notes.description: Any special instructions or modifications for this specific order item (e.g., 'no onions', 'extra cheese').

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a customer's review and rating for a restaurant or a specific menu item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the CustomerProfile who submitted the review. (Relationship: CustomerProfile 1:N Review)

restaurantId.description: Reference to the Restaurant being reviewed. (Relationship: Restaurant 1:N Review)

menuItemId.description: Optional reference to a specific MenuItem being reviewed. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N Review)

orderId.description: Optional reference to a specific Order associated with this review. (Relationship: Order 1:N Review)

rating.description: The rating given by the customer (typically on a scale of 1 to 5).

comment.description: The textual comment provided by the customer in their review.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the review was submitted.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the review was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual customer profiles. The `customerId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. Includes fields like `loyaltyPoints`, `walletBalance`, `referralCode`, and `favoriteMenuItemIds`.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the customer, also serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores multiple delivery/billing addresses for a specific customer. The `customerId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. The document includes denormalized `customerId` for authorization independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the customer, identifying the owner of this address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: Unique identifier for the address document.

definition.description: Stores public and administrative details for each restaurant. Publicly readable for menu browsing. Writeable only by associated `restaurantAdmins` or `platformAdmins`.

Stores individual menu items belonging to a specific restaurant. Publicly readable for customers. Writeable only by associated `restaurantAdmins` or `platformAdmins`. The document includes denormalized `restaurantId` for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The ID of the restaurant offering this menu item.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the menu item.

definition.description: Stores customer orders. Each order includes denormalized `customerId` and `restaurantId` for authorization independence, enabling customers to view their own orders and restaurant admins to view orders for their assigned restaurant without complex hierarchical rule lookups.

Stores individual items within an order. Each order item explicitly includes denormalized `customerId` and `restaurantId` (copied from the parent Order) to ensure authorization independence for all operations.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The ID of the parent order this item belongs to.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the order item.

definition.description: Stores customer reviews and ratings. Documents include denormalized `customerId` (for author-specific access) and `restaurantId` (for public display and admin viewing) for authorization independence.

A dedicated collection for platform-wide Super Admin roles. The existence of a document here (where `userId` is `request.auth.uid`) grants global administrative access, following DBAC principles. No custom claims are used.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user with Super Admin privileges.

definition.description: Defines users who are administrators for a specific restaurant. The existence of a document here (where `userId` is `request.auth.uid`) grants administrative access to the `{restaurantId}` specified in the path, following DBAC principles. No custom claims are used.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The ID of the restaurant this user is an admin for.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user assigned as an admin for this restaurant.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles: Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, DBAC (No Custom Claims), QAPs, and Invariants. This ensures a secure, scalable, and debuggable system.

1.  **Authorization Independence (via Denormalization):**
    *   For `CustomerProfile` and its subcollections (`addresses`), ownership is path-based (`/customerProfiles/{customerId}`). The `customerId` in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, eliminating `get()` calls for basic customer data access.
    *   For `Order` and `Review`, which have dual ownership (customer and restaurant), the documents themselves explicitly store `customerId` and `restaurantId` fields. This is a critical denormalization. Security rules for these collections can directly check `resource.data.customerId == request.auth.uid` or `isRestaurantAdmin(resource.data.restaurantId)`, avoiding `get()` calls on parent documents or other data documents for authorization.
    *   For `OrderItem`, which is a subcollection of `Order`, the `OrderItem` document will *also* denormalize `customerId` and `restaurantId` from its parent `Order` document. This allows atomic creation of an order with its items, and ensures rules for individual order items are authorization-independent.
    *   Restaurant-specific data (`MenuItem`) is structured under `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/menuItems/{menuItemId}`. The `restaurantId` is in the path, and denormalized into the `MenuItem` document, allowing rules to directly verify access without external `get()` calls on the parent `Restaurant` document.

2.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:**
    *   **Customer-Owned Data**: `/customerProfiles/{customerId}` and its subcollections like `/customerProfiles/{customerId}/addresses` ensure that `list` operations for a customer's private data are inherently secure. The query must match the authenticated user's ID in the path or document field, which rules can efficiently verify.
    *   **Publicly Accessible Data**: Collections like `/restaurants` and `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/menuItems` are structured to allow public read access for customers browsing menus. Access for writing or specific management is restricted by roles.
    *   **Shared/Collaborative Data (`Order`, `Review`)**: These are top-level collections (`/orders`, `/reviews`). While not strictly path-based for both `customerId` and `restaurantId`, their internal denormalized fields (`customerId`, `restaurantId`) enable robust QAPs. For instance, a customer can query `orders.where('customerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`, and a restaurant admin can query `orders.where('restaurantId', '==', adminRestaurantId)`. The security rules will enforce these filters by checking `resource.data.customerId` or `resource.data.restaurantId` respectively.
    *   **DBAC for Roles**: Separate collections are used for role assignments: `/platformAdmins/{userId}` for global super admins and `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/adminUsers/{userId}` for specific restaurant admins. This structural segregation makes authorization roles explicit and easily verifiable via `exists()` checks in security rules, supporting efficient `list` operations for role assignments and preventing arbitrary role modifications.

This architecture minimizes rule complexity, prevents common security pitfalls related to hierarchical data dependencies, and scales efficiently for global multi-tenant operations while supporting complex access patterns for different user personas.
